plugin.name: Know_Your_Audience
plugin.author: Claude
plugin.purpose: Reports know your audience results to party with resistance levels
plugin.description:
Know Your Audience parser for ZombieMUD.
Automatically parses "know your audience" spell results and reports to party: - Target health percentage - Weaknesses (most/least resisted damage types) - Resistance levels grouped by vulnerability (1-8 scale)
Resistance levels: 1 of 8 - completely vulnerable 2 of 8 - very vulnerable 3 of 8 - vulnerable 4 of 8 - susceptible 5 of 8 - somewhat susceptible 6 of 8 - barely susceptible 7 of 8 - immune 8 of 8 - invulnerable

muclient.script:
-- ==================== KNOW YOUR AUDIENCE SYSTEM ====================
-- Storage for know your audience data know_your_audience_data = know_your_audience_data or { target_name = nil, health_percent = nil, resistances = {}, most_resist = nil, least_resist = nil, collecting = false }
-- Mapping of resistance descriptions to numeric levels (1-8) local resistance_levels = { ["completely vulnerable"] = 1, ["very vulnerable"] = 2, ["vulnerable"] = 3, ["susceptible"] = 4, ["somewhat susceptible"] = 5, ["barely susceptible"] = 6, ["immune"] = 7, ["invulnerable"] = 8 }
-- Damage type abbreviations local damage_type_abbrev = { ["magical"] = "MAG", ["magic"] = "MAG", ["acid"] = "ACID", ["psionic"] = "PSI", ["electric"] = "ELEC", ["physical"] = "PHYS", ["fire"] = "FIRE", ["cold"] = "COLD", ["poison"] = "POIS", ["asphyxiation"] = "ASPHYX" }
function StartKnowYourAudience(name, line, wildcards) -- Extract target name from line like "Rune golem's gender is:" or "Dart's gender is: neuter" -- or "Off-duty guard's gender is:" or "O'Brien's gender is:" -- Pattern: match everything up to the last "'s gender is:" to handle names with apostrophes local target_name = string.match(line, "^(.+)'s gender is:") if target_name then know_your_audience_data.target_name = target_name:match("^%s*(.-)%s*$") -- trim whitespace know_your_audience_data.health_percent = nil know_your_audience_data.resistances = {} know_your_audience_data.most_resist = nil know_your_audience_data.least_resist = nil know_your_audience_data.collecting = true EnableTrigger("KnowYourAudienceEnd", true) end end
function CaptureKnowYourAudienceHealth(name, line, wildcards) -- Only process if we're collecting if not know_your_audience_data.collecting then return end
-- Pattern: "*The target is at about *% health." -- wildcards[1] = leading spaces, wildcards[2] = health percentage (e.g., "99%") local health = nil if wildcards and wildcards[2] then -- Remove % sign and convert to number health = tonumber(string.match(wildcards[2], "(%d+)")) if health then know_your_audience_data.health_percent = health return end end
-- Fallback: extract from line directly health = string.match(line, "about (%d+)%%") if health then know_your_audience_data.health_percent = tonumber(health) end end
function CaptureKnowYourAudienceResist(name, line, wildcards) if not know_your_audience_data.collecting then return end
-- Parse lines like "Rune golem is barely susceptible to acid." -- or "O'Brien is barely susceptible to acid." -- Pattern: match name (which may contain special chars), then " is ", then resistance level, then " to ", then damage type -- Use a more flexible pattern that handles names with special characters local pattern = " is (.+) to (.+)%." local level_desc, damage_type = string.match(line, pattern)
if level_desc and damage_type then level_desc = level_desc:lower():match("^%s*(.-)%s*$") -- trim and lowercase damage_type = damage_type:lower():match("^%s*(.-)%s*$") -- trim and lowercase
local level = resistance_levels[level_desc] if level then know_your_audience_data.resistances[damage_type] = level end end end
function CaptureKnowYourAudienceMostLeast(name, line, wildcards) if not know_your_audience_data.collecting then return end
-- Parse lines like " resists the damage type physical the most." -- Pattern: "*resists the damage type * the *." -- wildcards[1] = leading spaces, wildcards[2] = damage type, wildcards[3] = most/least if wildcards and wildcards[2] and wildcards[3] then local damage_type = string.lower(string.match(wildcards[2], "^%s*(.-)%s*$")) -- trim local most_or_least = string.lower(string.match(wildcards[3], "^%s*(.-)%s*$")) -- trim
if most_or_least == "most" then know_your_audience_data.most_resist = damage_type elseif most_or_least == "least" then know_your_audience_data.least_resist = damage_type end else -- Fallback to regex matching local damage_type, most_or_least = string.match(line, "damage type (%w+) the (%w+)%.") if damage_type and most_or_least then damage_type = damage_type:lower() most_or_least = most_or_least:lower()
if most_or_least == "most" then know_your_audience_data.most_resist = damage_type elseif most_or_least == "least" then know_your_audience_data.least_resist = damage_type end end end end
function EndKnowYourAudience(name, line, wildcards) if not know_your_audience_data.collecting then return end
-- Check if this is the alignment line (last line of know your audience) -- Pattern like "Rune golem is Neutral." or "Dart is Neutral." or "Off-duty guard is Neutral." -- or "O'Brien is Neutral." or "Smith, John is Neutral." local target_name = know_your_audience_data.target_name if not target_name then return end
-- Case-insensitive check for target name and alignment values -- Plain text search handles special characters (hyphens, apostrophes, commas) correctly local line_lower = string.lower(line) local target_lower = string.lower(target_name)
-- Check if line contains target name and " is " using plain text search local has_target = string.find(line_lower, target_lower, 1, true) -- plain text search (handles special chars) local has_is = string.find(line_lower, " is ", 1, true) -- plain text search
if not has_target or not has_is then return end
-- Check if it ends with one of the alignment values local alignments = { "angelic", "extremely good", "very good", "good", "slightly good", "neutral", "slightly evil", "evil", "very evil", "extremely evil", "demonic" }
local is_alignment = false for _, align in ipairs(alignments) do if string.find(line_lower, " is " .. align .. "%.") then is_alignment = true break end end
if is_alignment then know_your_audience_data.collecting = false EnableTrigger("KnowYourAudienceEnd", false) ProcessKnowYourAudienceResults() end end
function ProcessKnowYourAudienceResults() local data = know_your_audience_data
if not data.target_name then return end
-- Build resistance groups by level local resist_by_level = {} for damage_type, level in pairs(data.resistances) do if not resist_by_level[level] then resist_by_level[level] = {} end table.insert(resist_by_level[level], damage_type) end
-- Use scale of 8 as specified by user local scale_max = 8
-- Format target name (lowercase for consistency with example) local target_display = string.lower(data.target_name)
-- Build the summary message local health_str = "" if data.health_percent then health_str = target_display .. "'s at " .. data.health_percent .. "%" else health_str = target_display end
local most_str = "" local least_str = "" if data.most_resist then local abbrev = damage_type_abbrev[data.most_resist] or string.upper(data.most_resist:sub(1, 4)) most_str = "Most: " .. abbrev end if data.least_resist then local abbrev = damage_type_abbrev[data.least_resist] or string.upper(data.least_resist:sub(1, 4)) least_str = "Least: " .. abbrev end
local summary_parts = {} table.insert(summary_parts, health_str)
if least_str ~= "" and most_str ~= "" then table.insert(summary_parts, "Weaknesses: " .. least_str .. " - " .. most_str) elseif least_str ~= "" then table.insert(summary_parts, "Weaknesses: " .. least_str) elseif most_str ~= "" then table.insert(summary_parts, "Weaknesses: " .. most_str) end
-- Send summary line if #summary_parts > 0 then Execute("party say " .. table.concat(summary_parts, " - ")) end
-- Send separator Execute("party say " .. string.rep("=", 60))
-- Send resistance groups sorted by level (lowest to highest) local sorted_levels = {} for level, _ in pairs(resist_by_level) do table.insert(sorted_levels, level) end table.sort(sorted_levels)
for _, level in ipairs(sorted_levels) do local damage_types = resist_by_level[level] local abbrevs = {} for _, dt in ipairs(damage_types) do local abbrev = damage_type_abbrev[dt] or string.upper(dt:sub(1, 4)) table.insert(abbrevs, abbrev) end table.sort(abbrevs) -- Sort abbreviations alphabetically
Execute("party say " .. level .. " of " .. scale_max .. " - " .. table.concat(abbrevs, " ")) end
-- Reset data know_your_audience_data.target_name = nil know_your_audience_data.health_percent = nil know_your_audience_data.resistances = {} know_your_audience_data.most_resist = nil know_your_audience_data.least_resist = nil know_your_audience_data.collecting = false end
-- ==================== PLUGIN EVENTS ====================
function OnPluginInstall() -- Reset collecting state on reload so a mid-collection reload doesn't leave the end trigger enabled know_your_audience_data.collecting = false EnableTrigger("KnowYourAudienceEnd", false)
if IsPluginInstalled("94925710eda2ee22c3a5fd4a") and GetPluginVariable("94925710eda2ee22c3a5fd4a", "startup_mode") == "verbose" then ColourNote("green", "", "Know Your Audience plugin v1.0 loaded") ColourNote("cyan", "", " - Automatically parses know your audience results") ColourNote("cyan", "", " - Reports to party with resistance levels (1-8 scale)") end end
